title: Email Integration
summary: Email integration via IMAP (receive) and SMTP (send), covering Gmail app password setup, email threading headers, attachment handling, poll interval configuration, and anti-spam filtering by sender allowlist.
key_concepts: Email threading via In-Reply-To and References headers
Attachment handling: images to vision, text files to context
HTML vs plain text email body handling

env_vars.name: EMAIL_ALLOWED_SENDERS
env_vars.description: Comma-separated email addresses allowed to interact

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway including email IMAP listener

config_keys.description: Mark processed emails as seen (default: true)

code_examples.title: Gmail config with app password
code_examples.code:
email:
  imap_host: imap.gmail.com
  imap_port: 993
  smtp_host: smtp.gmail.com
  smtp_port: 587
  username: "myaddress@gmail.com"
  password: ""  # 16-char app password, not account password
  allowed_senders:
    - "trusted@example.com"

code_examples.title: Custom mailbox and poll interval
code_examples.code:
email:
  mailbox: "Hermes"
  poll_interval: 60  # check every 60 seconds
  mark_seen: true

code_examples.title: Gmail app password setup
code_examples.code:
# Gmail > Google Account > Security > 2-Step Verification > App passwords
# Select app: Mail, device: Other (Hermes)
# Copy the 16-character password (no spaces)

gotchas: Gmail requires an App Password (not your account password) when 2-Step Verification is enabled; regular password will fail
IMAP IDLE is not used; Hermes polls at poll_interval; there is inherent latency vs real-time platforms
mark_seen: true is required to avoid re-processing the same email on next poll; set false only for debugging
HTML email bodies are stripped to plain text before passing to the LLM; complex HTML layout is lost
Threading relies on proper In-Reply-To / References headers; broken threading in client apps can fork the conversation
Large attachments may hit memory limits; consider setting a max attachment size in email server settings
Sender allowlist uses exact email address matching; wildcards not supported
full_text:
## Email

Connect Hermes to an email account via IMAP (receive) and SMTP (send).

### Gmail setup

Gmail requires an App Password when 2-Step Verification is enabled:

1. Go to **Google Account → Security → 2-Step Verification → App passwords**.
2. Select app: **Mail**, device: **Other** (type Hermes).
3. Copy the 16-character password (no spaces).

Use this as your  — **not** your Google account password.

### Config

```yaml
email:
  imap_host: imap.gmail.com
  imap_port: 993
  smtp_host: smtp.gmail.com
  smtp_port: 587
  username: "myaddress@gmail.com"
  password: ""
  allowed_senders:
    - "trusted@example.com"
```

Start the gateway:

```bash
hermes gateway
```

Hermes polls the INBOX every 30 seconds (configurable via ).

### Email threading

Hermes maintains conversation context using email threading headers (, ). Replies stay in the same thread and carry forward conversation history.

### Attachments

- **Images** — forwarded to the vision pipeline if the active model supports vision.
- **Text files** (.txt, .md, .csv, .json) — content injected into the message context.
- **Other files** — filename and size noted but content not extracted.

### Custom mailbox

Monitor a specific folder instead of INBOX:

```yaml
email:
  mailbox: "Hermes"
  poll_interval: 60
```

### Allowlist

Only respond to emails from specific senders:

```yaml
email:
  allowed_senders:
    - "boss@company.com"
    - "client@example.com"
```

Emails from non-allowed senders are ignored (not bounced).

### Mark as seen

By default, processed emails are marked as seen to prevent re-processing:

```yaml
email:
  mark_seen: true  # default
```

Set  only for debugging — emails will be reprocessed every poll cycle.
